# plot_analytics_family_curves.py

<!-- Generated by scripts/docs/generate_api_docs.py. Do not edit manually. -->

## Purpose

Plot closed-form analytical win-rate curves for representative hybrid strategy families.

## CLI usage

```bash
python scripts/compare/plot_analytics_family_curves.py --help
```

## CLI options

| Option | Type | Default | Required | Choices | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| --dpi | int | 150 | False |  | f'PNG resolution. Default: {DEFAULT_DPI}' |
| --n2-values | int | 4 8 16 | False |  | n2 values to plot. Default: 4 8 16 |
| --optimal-search-summary-output | Path | None | False |  | Optional CSV path (or directory) for p_rule,best_success,best_candidate_name summary. |
| --output-dir | Path | DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR | False |  | f'Directory to save PNG figures. Default: {DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR}' |
| --search-families | str | majority pyramid horizontal vertical | False | majority pyramid horizontal vertical | Families to include in optimal analytical search. |
| --search-k-box-values | int | None | False |  | Optional search k_box values. Defaults by plot n2: n2=4 -> 1 3; n2=8 -> 1 3 7; n2=16 -> 1 3 7 15. |
| --search-n2-values | int | None | False |  | Optional search n2 values. Default: same n2 as current plot. |
| --show | flag | False | False |  | Display figures interactively after saving. |
| --show-optimal-search | flag | False | False |  | Overlay optimal analytical search envelope on each figure. |
